title: Surfshark Review 2026: Still the Best Value VPN?
description: Surfshark review 2026. Unlimited devices, CleanWeb, Nexus network, and solid streaming support. Find out if it's still the top pick for value-conscious users.
content:
## Surfshark Review 2026

Surfshark launched in 2018 and immediately made a name for itself with one feature that competitors ignored: **unlimited simultaneous connections**. While NordVPN capped you at six devices and ExpressVPN at five, Surfshark said connect every device you own. That positioning worked. By 2026, Surfshark is one of the top three VPNs in the world and costs less than most of its rivals. But does it hold up in practice?

This review covers what you actually get in 2026, where it falls short, and who should pick it.

## Overview: What You Pay and What You Get

Surfshark's pricing structure in 2026:

- **Monthly plan:** around $15.45/month (expensive, not recommended)
- **1-year plan:** around $3.99/month
- **2-year plan:** around $2.49/month

The 2-year plan is where the value is. At roughly $2.49/month you get unlimited devices, access to 3,200+ servers in 100+ countries, a no-logs policy, and a suite of features we'll cover below. That's cheaper than most streaming subscriptions.

Surfshark uses **WireGuard** (fast, modern), **OpenVPN** (trusted, slightly slower), and **IKEv2** (good on mobile). The default on most platforms is WireGuard, and you'll rarely need to touch the protocol settings.

## Speed Tests: Decent, But Not the Fastest

Surfshark is fast enough for every day tasks. Streaming 4K, gaming, video calls, torrenting -- all of these work without noticeable lag on nearby servers.

That said, if you run a head-to-head speed test against **ExpressVPN** or **NordVPN**, Surfshark often comes in third. Real-world numbers vary, but expect a 10-20% drop from your baseline connection on close servers. On distant servers (say, connecting to Singapore from Europe), the drop can be more pronounced.

For most households this won't matter. You won't notice the difference between 400 Mbps and 350 Mbps. If you run a business that uploads large files or you're a competitive gamer, ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol has an edge. For everyone else, Surfshark is fast enough.

## CleanWeb: The Built-In Ad and Malware Blocker

CleanWeb is Surfshark's ad blocker, tracker blocker, and malware shield baked directly into the VPN client. You toggle it on once and forget about it.

In practice, CleanWeb blocks most display ads, blocks known malware domains before your browser even reaches them, and strips a lot of the cross-site tracking that follows you around the web. It won't replace a dedicated browser extension like uBlock Origin for power users, but for casual users who want one tool that does everything, CleanWeb is a genuine selling point.

It works at the DNS level, which means it covers all apps on your device, not just your browser. That's different from browser extensions and more useful on phones.

## Nexus: The Shared IP Network

Nexus is Surfshark's proprietary routing layer. Instead of sending your traffic through a single server, Nexus routes it through their entire network via a shared IP pool. The practical benefit is that your IP address changes continuously, making it harder for sites to track you based on a static IP.

Nexus also includes **IP Rotator** (periodically changes your IP during a session without dropping the connection), **IP Randomizer** (assigns a random IP from the server country on each new connection), and **Multi-Hop** (chains two servers for extra anonymity).

Most users will leave Nexus on its default setting and never think about it. Power users who want maximum anonymity will appreciate the granular options.

## Bypasser: Split Tunneling That Works

Bypasser is Surfshark's name for split tunneling. It lets you choose which apps or websites go through the VPN and which connect directly.

Common use cases:

- Your bank app flags the VPN and blocks you. Add it to Bypasser's exclusion list.
- You want to stream local TV through the VPN but keep your online banking on your real IP.
- A work tool requires your real IP address while you want everything else protected.

Bypasser works on Windows and Android. On iOS and macOS, full split tunneling is limited due to platform restrictions. If split tunneling on iOS is a hard requirement, NordVPN handles this slightly better.

## Streaming: Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+

Surfshark's streaming support is one of its strongest points. It unblocks:

- **Netflix US, UK, DE, JP** and several other libraries reliably
- **BBC iPlayer** (hit or miss -- some servers work, some don't, but the ones that do work consistently)
- **Disney+** across most regions
- **Amazon Prime Video**, **Hulu**, **HBO Max**

The trick is using servers labeled for streaming. Surfshark's app marks these clearly. When a server stops working for a specific service, Surfshark usually fixes it within a few days. Their support team responds quickly to streaming reports.

For streaming purposes, Surfshark is on par with NordVPN and behind ExpressVPN only marginally. It beats most other VPNs on this list easily. See our [ExpressVPN vs NordVPN 2026 comparison](/blog/expressvpn-vs-nordvpn-2026) for how those two stack up directly.

## Privacy: No-Logs Audited by Deloitte

Surfshark's no-logs policy was audited by **Deloitte in 2023**. Deloitte reviewed the infrastructure and policy documentation and confirmed Surfshark does not store connection logs, IP addresses, browsing history, or session data.

This matters because many VPNs claim to be no-logs but have never had an independent audit. Surfshark has, and the audit report is publicly available.

Surfshark is incorporated in the **Netherlands**, which is in the EU and subject to GDPR. This is neither ideal nor terrible for privacy. The Netherlands participates in intelligence-sharing arrangements, but that only matters if a VPN actually stores logs. Surfshark's audited policy says they don't.

For most users, the Deloitte audit combined with the no-logs architecture is enough. If you're a journalist or activist in a high-risk environment, consider pairing any VPN with Tor for the most sensitive activities.

If you're still learning what a VPN actually does under the hood, our [beginner's guide](/guides/what-is-a-vpn) covers the basics clearly.

## What Surfshark Does Less Well

No product is perfect. Here's where Surfshark falls short:

**Speed ceiling.** As mentioned, ExpressVPN and NordVPN edge it out on raw speed. Not a problem for most households, but worth knowing.

**iOS split tunneling.** Bypasser doesn't have full functionality on iOS. Apple's VPN framework restricts what third-party apps can do with split tunneling.

**Server counts vs. server quality.** 3,200+ servers sounds impressive, but some of those are virtual locations (the server is physically in a different country than the listed location). Surfshark is transparent about which are virtual, but it's worth checking if your target location matters.

**Antivirus add-on costs extra.** Surfshark's antivirus (Surfshark One bundle) is a paid upgrade on top of the base plan. NordVPN and Bitdefender bundle antivirus more cleanly.

## The Verdict: Best for Households and Multi-Device Users

Surfshark is the best value VPN in 2026 for anyone who needs to protect multiple devices on a budget. The unlimited connections policy is unique. The 2-year price is genuinely low. The feature set (CleanWeb, Nexus, Bypasser, solid streaming support) covers almost everything a typical household needs.

Who should pick Surfshark:

- You have 5+ devices (phones, tablets, laptops, smart TV, router)
- You want ad blocking and malware protection built in
- You stream Netflix or BBC iPlayer regularly
- You're sharing the subscription with family members
- You want an audited no-logs policy without paying premium prices

Who should look elsewhere:

- You need the absolute fastest VPN for large uploads (ExpressVPN)
- You want advanced features like Tor-over-VPN (NordVPN's Onion Over VPN)
- Full iOS split tunneling is a hard requirement
- You need a server in a very specific niche country

For most people in 2026, Surfshark is the right call. The price is hard to beat, the features are solid, and the Deloitte audit gives you confidence that the no-logs policy is real.
